image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Science reveals how anchoring bias controls your choices more than you think.
Context visual: Anchoring bias happens when we rely too heavily on the first piece of information we receive.
Escalation visual: Imagine seeing a $1,000 TV, then a $500 one. That $500 looks like a steal, right?
Evidence visual: Studies show people are more likely to buy when they're anchored to higher prices.
Payoff visual: Being aware of this bias can help you make smarter choices, not just in shopping, but in life.
Cta visual: Want to learn more about your mind? Hit that follow button!
